channel.title: BBC Sport
channel.copyright: Copyright: (C) British Broadcasting Corporation, see https://www.bbc.co.uk/usingthebbc/terms-of-use/#15metadataandrssfeeds for terms and conditions of reuse.

item.title: Man City make statement in dominant win over Chelsea
item.description: Manchester City put three goals past Chelsea in a 17-minute spell at Stamford Bridge, to close the gap on leaders Arsenal to six points with a game in hand in the Premier League.

item.title: Sunderland sink Spurs in De Zerbi's opening game in charge
item.description: Sunderland's Nordi Mukiele scores the only goal in the Black Cats 1-0 home win against Tottenham, handing Roberto De Zerbi a loss in his first game in charge as Tottenham manager. The result leaves Spurs in the Premier League relegation zone.

item.title: Forest hit back to earn crucial point against Villa
item.description: Neco Williams scores his first goal since September to earn a 1-1 draw for Nottingham Forest against Aston Villa at the City Ground.

item.title: Stoppage-time Mateta penalty seals comeback win for Palace against Newcastle
item.description: Jean-Philippe Mateta comes off the bench to score a late double, including a 94th-minute penalty, as Crystal Palace come back to beat Newcastle United 2-1 at Selhurst Park.

item.title: Man City smell blood - why Arsenal should fear title rivals
item.description: Manchester City capitalise on Arsenal's latest slip-up in style - and now Pep Guardiola's side must be feared as the Premier League title race hots up.

item.title: Will Romero's tears become the enduring image of Tottenham's season?
item.description: Cristian Romero's tears at Sunderland could prove the lasting image of Tottenham's season if it ends with relegation, writes Phil McNulty.

item.title: Will thrilling Scottish title race be decided on goal difference?
item.description: Rangers' 6-3 win over Falkirk extends the Ibrox side's advantage over leaders Heart of Midlothian and Celtic should the Scottish Premiership title be decided on goal difference.

item.title: 'I'm not blind' - Howe 'accountable' for loss after three-week break
item.description: Eddie Howe says he is "accountable" for Newcastle's defeat by Crystal Palace after three weeks without a game.

item.title: New Mainoo deal closer, says Man Utd boss Carrick
item.description: Manchester United midfielder Kobbie Mainoo is getting closer to agreeing a new contract with the club, head coach Michael Carrick says.

item.title: Newcastle want Man Utd's Ugarte - Monday's gossip
item.description: Newcastle want Manchester United's Manuel Ugarte, Liverpool face a battle with Chelsea for Eli Junior Kroupi, plus more.

item.title: Wrexham not down and out yet - Parkinson
item.description: Phil Parkinson says his side are still in the mix to earn a Championship play-off place, despite their blunt showing in a 2-0 loss to Birmingham City.

item.title: What is going on with Garnacho?
item.description: Alejandro Garnacho is struggling to stand out at Chelsea - so can the £40m signing from Manchester United still realise his potential at Stamford Bridge?

item.title: Union Berlin appoint first female boss in Europe's top men's leagues
item.description: Marie-Louise Eta becomes the first woman appointed to manage a men's team in one of Europe's top five leagues as she is named interim head coach of Bundesliga side Union Berlin.

item.title: Four months and counting - De Ligt's wait for Man Utd return goes on
item.description: Manchester United defender Matthijs de Ligt has been out for over four months with a back injury and still doesn't know when he will return.

item.title: I like proving the doubters wrong, says England's Blindkilde Brown
item.description: England midfielder Laura Blindkilde Brown says she "likes proving people wrong" after becoming a regular starter at Manchester City this season.

item.title: Blackpool fan dies following medical emergency
item.description: Blackpool Football Club pay tribute to supporter Graham Sharman who died following a medical emergency at Bloomfield Road on Saturday.

item.title: Tottenham players don't need a coach - De Zerbi
item.description: Tottenham manager Roberto De Zerbi says he needs to be a "big brother" and "father" figure to his players after their 1-0 defeat at Sunderland in his first game in charge leaves his side in the bottom three of the Premier League.

item.title: 'Big punch in the face' - could Arsenal really blow title from here?
item.description: Nine points clear with six games to play - could Arsenal still really blow the Premier League title from that position?

item.title: Fortune favours the brave - will Slot start Ngumoha against PSG?
item.description: Liverpool's 17-year-old star Rio Ngumoha stole the show against Fulham - will Arne Slot now start him against PSG?

item.title: Farke says FA Cup buzz can lift Leeds for relegation fight
item.description: Leeds United head coach Daniel Farke dismisses suggestions the FA Cup could distract his side from their Premier League relegation battle, as they prepare to face Manchester United.

item.title: Title race over if Man City don't beat Arsenal - Guardiola
item.description: Manchester City manager Pep Guardiola praises Nico O'Reilly in their 3-0 win over Chelsea but says their Premier League title challenge will be over next Sunday if they fail to beat "extraordinary" Arsenal.

item.title: Palace kept believing in Newcastle win - Glasner
item.description: Crystal Palace manager Oliver Glasner says he wants to keep his team's forward momentum going after Jean-Philippe Mateta came off the bench to score a late double in their 2-1 win over Newcastle United in the Premier League.

item.title: Newcastle defeat to Palace 'deflating' - Howe
item.description: Newcastle United manager Eddie Howe says his sides 2-1 defeat by Crystal Palace was "deflating" and calls the decision to award a late penalty against them "soft", as The Magpies fall to 14th in the Premier League.

item.title: Villa are 'motivated and excited' by Champions League prospect - Emery
item.description: Aston Villa manager Unai Emery says his side are feeling "motivated and excited" at the prospect of qualifying for the Champions League next season, after their 1-1 draw away to Nottingham Forest in the Premier League.

item.title: Chelsea need to improve their resilience - Rosenior
item.description: Chelsea manager Liam Rosenior admits his side need to "improve their resilience in difficult moments" and focus on winning their next league game against Manchester United to stay in contention for a Champions League spot.

item.title: Top 10 is a good target for Sunderland - Le Bris
item.description: Sunderland manager Regis le Bris believes his side can secure a top half finish in the table come the end of the season, after Sunderland's 1-0 win over Tottenham Hotspur moved them to 10th in the Premier League.

item.title: No Semenyo, no problem? How Bournemouth continue to defy the odds
item.description: Bournemouth have not lost a game since star forward Antoine Semenyo left for Manchester City in January - how have they done it?

item.title: Ipswich looking good but Foxes look doomed - Aaron McLean's EFL takeaways
item.description: Final Score EFL pundit Aaron McLean reflects on the highs and lows after Saturday's action.

item.title: Wood's return from injury feels like a new signing - Pereira
item.description: Nottingham Forest manager Vitor Pereira praises Chris Wood's character and connection with his team-mates, after the New Zealand striker returned from a six-month injury lay-off in their 1-1 draw with Aston Villa.

item.title: 'He brings an energy' - Should Ngumoha start against PSG?
item.description: Alan Shearer and Danny Murphy debate whether 17-year-old Rio Ngumoha should start for Liverpool in their Champions League quarter-final second leg against PSG, after starring and scoring in the Reds' 2-0 win over Fulham.

item.title: Ngumoha getting 'stronger and stronger' - Slot
item.description: Arne Slot says Rio Ngumoha showed "how important his quality is" after the 17-year-old scores during Liverpool's 2-0 victory over Fulham at Anfield.

item.title: Tottenham will go down - Sutton
item.description: Football Focus pundits Chris Sutton and Steph Houghton discuss whether Roberto de Zerbi can improve Spurs and keep them in the Premier League.

item.title: Everything he does is impressive - Shearer lauds Igor Thiago
item.description: Match of the Day's Alan Shearer analyses the performance of Brentford's Igor Thiago, as the Brazilian striker took his Premier League tally to 21 goals with a brace in his side's 2-2 draw with Everton.

item.title: Fulham 'cannot repeat' first-half display - Silva
item.description: Fulham manager Marco Silva criticises his sides first-half display, after conceding twice in four minutes against Liverpool.

item.title: Sutton's predictions v boxing promoter & Arsenal fan Frank Warren
item.description: Chris Sutton takes on boxing promoter Frank Warren plus the BBC readers and AI with his predictions for this weekend's Premier League fixtures.

item.title: West Ham have fresh hope - but Spurs now in uncharted territory
item.description: West Ham's thumping win over Wolves not only boosts their own Premier League survival hopes, but plunges Tottenham into the drop zone - and to new depths.

item.title: We asked fans of Premier League clubs how they are feeling - find out the results
item.description: As the Premier League returns after a three-week hiatus, we asked fans how they are feeling about their club.

item.title: It makes English football unique - why the Football League is important too
item.description: Tony Pulis explains why the English Football League is so important and why he loved life in the lower divisions, as well as the lessons he learned.

item.title: 54 years, 48 leagues, 2,000 grounds - the ultimate football odyssey
item.description: BBC Sport travels with the UK's ultimate groundhopper to the final match of a decades-long, history-making journey.

item.title: 'You need to have arrogance' - Rooney and Littler on being number one
item.description: Wayne Rooney and Luke Littler both know what it is like to be thrust into the limelight at a young age.

item.title: Seven games to go - how will De Zerbi try to save Spurs?
item.description: Clubs at risk of relegation tend to hire pragmatic coaches - but Spurs have not done that by appointing Roberto de Zerbi.

item.title: How are the Scottish Premiership post-split fixtures decided?
item.description: The Scottish Premiership split has again been a controversial talking point, so why does it exist and why is it so complicated?

item.title: How WSL aims to bridge academy gap - and why it needs to
item.description: Chelsea manager Sonia Bompastor raised concerns over the gap between academy football and the first team - so is it an issue in the game and how could it be solved?

item.title: All February and March's done deals, including deadline day
item.description: Check out the significant signings and departures in the Premier League, Scottish Premiership, EFL and Women's Super League.

item.title: How to follow the FA Cup quarter-finals on the BBC
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything team explain how you can watch, listen and follow the FA Cup quarter-finals.

item.title: How does Scottish Premiership split work?
item.description: The Scottish Premiership title race has been a hot topic all season and it's about to become even more interesting with the league's split.

item.title: How does Tudor's record compare to other Tottenham bosses?
item.description: Our Ask Me Anything team compare Igor Tudor's managerial record to other Tottenham managers of recent years.

item.title: Have all home nations ever played in same World Cup?
item.description: With England and Scotland already qualified for the 2026 World Cup, Wales and Northern Ireland both face European qualifiers in March.

item.title: Who is one card away from suspension in the Premier League?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything team looks at who is currently nearing a suspension in the Premier League.

item.title: All you need to know about 2027 Women's World Cup
item.description: Our Ask Me Anything team take a look at everything you need to know for the 2027 Women's World Cup.

item.title: When can Arsenal win the Premier League?
item.description: Arsenal are nine points clear at the top of the Premier League, but when can they officially win the title?

item.title: Are these the 30 minutes that saved Hearts' title bid?
item.description: As Heart of Midlothian fight back to beat Motherwell, it could prove to be the 30 minutes that saved their Scottish title chances.

item.title: Spalletti signs new Juventus contract amid Italy links
item.description: Juventus manager Luciano Spalletti signs a two-year contract extension with the club, effectively ruling him out of a return to the Italy national team.

item.title: How fluid PSG exposed Slot's back-five tweak
item.description: Arne Slot changed Liverpool's shape for their Champions League quarter-final first leg - but their tactics were still bested by European champions Paris St-Germain. Umir Irfan explains how.

item.title: After more than 14 years at Atletico Madrid, what next for Simeone?
item.description: BBC Sport asks if Atletico Madrid could really part ways with manager Diego Simeone at the end of the season?

item.title: Napoli warn they could punish Lukaku over training no-show
item.description: Striker Romelu Lukaku could face disciplinary action after Napoli released a statement to say he has ignored a request to return to the club.

item.title: How former Liverpool prospect found love for football again
item.description: After 18 years at Liverpool, Tyler Morton tells BBC Sport how moving to Lyon in the summer has helped him fall back in love with football.

item.title: From shame to pride - inside Europe's most exciting league
item.description: Poland's top flight has become Europe's most exciting league. Guillem Balague looks at how they have achieved it.

item.title: New faces pushing Wales - and why game in Wrexham is so important
item.description: In her debut BBC Sport column, Rhiannon Roberts discusses Wales' World Cup qualifying double-header with Albania, the changing of the guard within the squad, and excitement at playing for her country in Wrexham.

item.title: Wales told to show up in Albania qualifiers
item.description: Rhian Wilkinson tells Wales they must "show up" in their Women's World Cup qualifying double-header against Albania in bid to finish top of group.

item.title: Liverpool to sign Germany forward Endemann in July
item.description: Liverpool have reached an agreement to sign Germany international Vivien Endemann upon the expiry of her contract at Wolfsburg.

item.title: Uncapped Chambers deserves NI chance - Bell
item.description: Northern Ireland midfielder Megan Bell says that she is "really delighted" that uncapped Linfield striker Cora Chambers has been included in the squad for the 2027 World Cup qualifiers against Malta.

item.title: When football turned to penalties to end 'cruel' system
item.description: No-one knew what to expect at football's first official penalty shootout - and no-one wanted to go down in history as the first to miss.

item.title: Frank's downfall: Inside his struggle to get players and fans onboard
item.description: Thomas Frank has been sacked as Tottenham manager after just eight months in charge. BBC Sport's Sami Mokbel takes a closer look at his tenure.

item.title: Cages, crushes and stabbings - is European away safety getting worse?
item.description: BBC Sport explores the safety issues fans face when following their team across Europe.

item.title: The great number nine decline - where have England's strikers gone?
item.description: BBC Sport takes an in-depth look at why England lack centre-forward options behind Harry Kane - a far cry from when the likes of Les Ferdinand only won 17 caps.

item.title: Captain, player, manager, Mister, number one - Davids at Barnet
item.description: BBC Sport remembers Edgar Davids' 15 months as Barnet player-manager in League Two and the Conference.

item.title: From farming village to Liverpool icon - the Salah story
item.description: BBC Sport visits Egypt to discover what Mohamed Salah means to the people of the football-mad country, and how a small boy from humble beginnings became a national icon.

item.title: Napoli 'smell blood' & target Premier League's discarded stars
item.description: After Scott McTominay was a hit, Napoli went back to the Premier League for Kevin de Bruyne, could more players follow them to Serie A?
